title: JavaScript Numbers Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of the essential properties and tools for creating robust and cross-browser web experiences with numerical values and logic.

questions.question: Which of these is the most modern and recommended way to build a professional-grade web application in JavaScript for handling very, very large numbers (greater than 9 quadrillion)?
options: Table Layouts
Float Layouts
The BigInt data type.
Absolute Positioning only

questions.question: Which of these is a major 'Pro' of using .toFixed(2) compared to a regular number variable?
options: It makes your HTML files smaller and more readable.
It rounds a number into a string with a specific number of decimal places, which is essential for things like currency (e.g., $10.00).
It is always supported by every browser.
It replaces the need for any HTML5 tags.

questions.question: What is 'NaN' inside a professional web application number calculation?
options: To make the site load much faster.
A special value that stands for 'Not-a-Number', which is returned when math is performed on a non-numeric value.
To choose the colors for the animation.
To automatically generate CSS animations.

questions.question: Why is it important to almost ALWAYS avoid 'Universal Selectors' everywhere in your professional JavaScript number processing?
options: They are difficult to maintain.
They can make the site load much slower and potentially confuse the number logic.
They are not supported in modern browsers.
They take up no space in the browser.

questions.question: Which of these is a best practice for a professional-style stylesheet for JavaScript numbers for financial applications?
options: Commenting every single line of code.
Using a reset or normalize stylesheet.
Converting all values to cents (integers) to avoid floating-point math issues, and then converting back only for display.
Using variables for colors and spacing.
